title: bun-types pinned to exact canary build while engines allows broad version range
reasoning: The engines field permits bun >=1.4.0-canary.1 (including stable 1.4.0+), but bun-types is pinned to an exact canary snapshot (1.4.0-canary.20260519T150915). Contributors using a different bun build (e.g., stable 1.4.x or a later canary) will have type definitions that may not match their runtime APIs. While this does not break vitest execution directly (vitest transpiles without type-checking), it causes the lint script's `tsc --noEmit` to produce spurious errors or miss real type issues, creating friction in the test-adjacent dev workflow and CI pipelines that run lint+test together.
reproduction: Install a stable bun >=1.4.0 release, run `bun install`, then `bun run lint`. Any APIs that changed between the pinned canary and the stable release will produce type errors unrelated to actual code correctness.
recommendation: Either widen bun-types to a caret range tracking stable releases (e.g., ^1.4.0) once stable is available, or narrow engines to the exact canary build so contributors know which runtime to use. Document the required bun version in the README if a canary is intentionally required.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Vitest transpiles TypeScript without type-checking, so type mismatches between bun-types and the runtime never surface during `bun run test`. Only the lint script (tsc --noEmit) exercises these types.
minimumFixScope: Update the bun-types devDependency version specifier or the engines constraint so they are consistent.
